claims.source_quote: actual rainfall received was 176.2 mm, which is 25.1 percent above normal rainfall
claims.claim: Monsoon 2016 rainfall was above the normal average by 25.1 percent.

claims.source_quote: actual rainfall received was 3.0 mm, which was 88.6 percent below the normal rainfall
claims.claim: Post-monsoon 2016 rainfall was 88.6 percent below normal.

claims.source_quote: actual rainfall received was 75.5 mm, which was 1.6 percent above the normal
claims.claim: Winter 2017 rainfall was slightly above the normal average.

claims.source_quote: canal head withdrawals stood at 71.35 million acre feet (MAF) showing an increase of 9 percent
claims.claim: Kharif 2016 canal head withdrawals increased by 9 percent over the same period of the previous year.

comparison.baseline: 65.47 MAF during same period last year

claims.source_quote: canal head withdrawals decreased by 10 percent and stood at 29.66 MAF
claims.claim: Rabi 2016-17 canal head withdrawals decreased by 10 percent compared with the same period last year.

comparison.baseline: 32.90 MAF during same period last year

claims.source_quote: aimed at meeting the widening gap between demand and supply
claims.claim: Water sector strategies are directed toward closing a widening demand-supply gap through demand management and enhanced water availability.
claims.constraint_or_risk: widening gap between demand and supply

claims.source_quote: Rs.31.716 billion was allocated for water sector’s development projects/ programmes
claims.claim: Rs.31.716 billion was allocated for water sector development projects and programmes in FY 2016-17, with more than Rs.24.00 billion expected to be utilized by end-June 2017.

claims.source_quote: Major Water Sector Projects under Implementation
claims.claim: Major water sector projects under implementation included dams, canals, and drainage schemes across Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Sindh, Balochistan, and FATA areas.
claims.actor: Ministry of Planning, Development and Reform
actors: Ministry of Planning, Development and Reform
claims.policy_or_programme: Major Water Sector Projects under Implementation
claims.geography: Pakistan; Khyber Pakhtunkhwa; Sindh; Balochistan; FATA

claims.source_quote: Substantial completion (phase-I) of Kachhi Canal in Balochistan & Rainee Canal Sindh
claims.claim: Phase-I of Kachhi Canal in Balochistan and Rainee Canal in Sindh were substantially completed to irrigate 0.168 million acres.
claims.effect: irrigating 0.168 million acres

claims.source_quote: Completion of Gomal Zam Dam in FATA/Khyber Pakhtunkhwa and Darwat Dam (Sindh).
claims.claim: Gomal Zam Dam and Darwat Dam were completed during FY 2016-17 landmarks.

claims.source_quote: Rs 1,000 million will be expended for on-going works of Nai Gaj dam
claims.claim: Rs.1,000 million was planned for ongoing Nai Gaj Dam works to irrigate 28,800 acres and generate 4.20 MW.
claims.effect: irrigate 28,800 acres of land and power generation of 4.20 MW

claims.source_quote: Rs 900 million will be utilized for lining of small canals & minors
claims.claim: About Rs.900 million was planned for lining small canals and minors in Punjab and Sindh to save water losses in the existing irrigation system.
claims.policy_or_programme: lining of small canals and minors
policy_or_programmes: lining of small canals and minors
claims.constraint_or_risk: water losses of existing irrigation system
claims.effect: save water losses

claims.source_quote: water availability at farm gate will remain about 132.70 MAF
claims.claim: The water sector development programme was expected to keep farm-gate water availability at about 132.70 MAF.
claims.actor: Ministry of Planning, Development and Reform
actors: Ministry of Planning, Development and Reform
claims.effect: farm-gate water availability will remain about 132.70 MAF

claims.source_quote: Rs. 4.80 billion are expected to be utilized for the implementation of RBOD-I, II & III Projects
claims.claim: Rs.4.80 billion was expected to be used for RBOD-I, RBOD-II, and RBOD-III to protect and reclaim waterlogged and salt-affected irrigated land and address Manchar Lake pollution.
claims.actor: Ministry of Planning, Development and Reform
actors: Ministry of Planning, Development and Reform
claims.constraint_or_risk: waterlogged and salt effected irrigated land; Manchar Lake water pollution
claims.effect: protect and reclaim waterlogged and salt affected irrigated land and Manchar Lake for water pollution

claims.source_quote: ACAC has set the indicative agricultural credit disbursement targets of Rs700 billion
claims.claim: The Agriculture Credit Advisory Committee set an indicative agricultural credit disbursement target of Rs.700 billion for FY 2016-17 across 52 participating institutions.

comparison.baseline: last year’s disbursement of Rs 598.3 billion

claims.source_quote: banks disbursed Rs.473.1 billion which is 67.6 percent of the overall annual target
claims.claim: During July-March FY 2016-17, banks disbursed Rs.473.1 billion in agricultural credit, achieving 67.6 percent of the annual target and exceeding the corresponding previous-year disbursement by 22.7 percent.

comparison.baseline: Rs.385.5 billion during corresponding period last year

claims.source_quote: the overall performance of banks remained encouraging and witnessed 22.7 percent growth
claims.claim: Despite real-sector challenges, banks' agricultural credit performance was described as encouraging due to 22.7 percent disbursement growth.
claims.effect: 22.7 percent growth in disbursement

claims.source_quote: Ten Microfinance banks have disbursed Rs.61.3 billion and as a group has already surpassed their annual targets
claims.claim: Microfinance banks surpassed their annual agricultural credit targets during July-March FY 2016-17.

claims.subsection: Box-2: Credit Disbursement to Farm and Non-Farm Sector
claims.source_quote: farm sector has received Rs.232.0 billion or 49 percent while non-farm sector absorbed Rs.241.1 billion
claims.claim: Non-farm agricultural credit slightly exceeded farm-sector credit during July-March FY 2016-17.

comparison.baseline: farm sector Rs.232.0 billion or 49 percent
comparison.comparator: non-farm sector Rs.241.1 billion or 51 percent

claims.subsection: Box-2: Credit Disbursement to Farm and Non-Farm Sector
claims.source_quote: mainly due to more opportunities for Agricultural Value Chain Financing (AVCF)
claims.claim: The increasing trend in non-farm credit was mainly attributed to more opportunities for Agricultural Value Chain Financing, especially in livestock, dairy, and poultry.
claims.cause: more opportunities for Agricultural Value Chain Financing (AVCF) especially in Livestock/Dairy and Poultry sectors
claims.effect: increasing trend of non-farm sector

claims.subsection: SBP’s Initiatives for the Promotion of Agriculture Financing
claims.source_quote: initiatives have been taken to remove the bottlenecks and enhance access to financial services
claims.claim: SBP and stakeholders took policy and regulatory initiatives to remove bottlenecks and improve farmers' access to financial services, especially for smaller farmers.
claims.policy_or_programme: SBP’s Initiatives for the Promotion of Agriculture Financing
policy_or_programmes: SBP’s Initiatives for the Promotion of Agriculture Financing
claims.constraint_or_risk: bottlenecks in access to financial services
claims.effect: enhance access to financial services for the farmers, especially smaller ones

claims.subsection: SBP’s Initiatives for the Promotion of Agriculture Financing
claims.source_quote: SBP issued draft framework for Warehouse Receipt Financing
claims.claim: SBP issued a draft Warehouse Receipt Financing framework to support specialized financing products for farmers and value-chain participants.
claims.cause: objective of improving the performance of the agriculture sector and developing commodities physical trade and marketing system
claims.effect: facilitates banks in development of specialized products for providing financing

claims.source_quote: providing 50 percent credit risk coverage to Participating Financial Institutions
claims.claim: The Credit Guarantee Scheme for Small and Marginalized Farmers provides 50 percent credit-risk coverage to participating financial institutions for loans up to Rs.100,000 to small farmers with limited landholdings.
claims.indicator_or_subject: Credit Guarantee Scheme for Small and Marginalized Farmers
claims.policy_or_programme: Credit Guarantee Scheme for Small and Marginalized Farmers (CGSMF)
policy_or_programmes: Credit Guarantee Scheme for Small and Marginalized Farmers (CGSMF)
claims.constraint_or_risk: small farmers do not have adequate collateral acceptable to banks
claims.cause: small farmers do not have adequate collateral
claims.effect: sensitize financial institutions to lend to small farmers and meet working capital requirements

claims.source_quote: introduced CLIS in 2008 for five major crops
claims.claim: The Crop Loan Insurance Scheme covers five major crops to mitigate default risk from natural calamities and provide repayment assurance to banks.
claims.constraint_or_risk: default risk due to natural calamities
claims.effect: mitigating default risk and provide repayment assurance to banks

claims.source_quote: covers all livestock loans up to Rs 5 million for purchase of animals
claims.claim: The Livestock Loan Insurance Scheme covers livestock loans up to Rs.5 million for animal purchases and protects borrowers against animal death from specified risks.
claims.constraint_or_risk: death of animals due to disease, natural death due to flood, heavy rains, wind storm and accidental death
claims.effect: borrowers are covered against death of animals

claims.source_quote: Livestock contributed approximately 58.3 percent to the agriculture value added and 11.4 percent to the overall GDP
claims.claim: Livestock contributed 58.3 percent to agriculture value added and 11.4 percent to overall GDP in 2016-17.
claims.indicator_or_subject: livestock contribution to value added and GDP

comparison.baseline: 58.3 percent agriculture value added and 11.6 percent GDP last year
comparison.comparator: 58.3 percent agriculture value added and 11.4 percent GDP in 2016-17

claims.source_quote: increased from Rs. 1288 billion (2015-16) to Rs. 1333 billion (2016-17)
claims.claim: Livestock gross value addition at constant 2005-06 factor cost increased by 3.4 percent in 2016-17 over the previous year.

claims.source_quote: Nearly 8 million families are involved in livestock raising deriving more than 35 percent income
claims.claim: Livestock is central to the rural economy, with nearly 8 million families deriving more than 35 percent of income from livestock production activities.
claims.effect: source of cash income for rural and marginal people

claims.source_quote: Estimated Livestock Population
claims.claim: Livestock population figures for 2014-15 to 2016-17 are estimated using inter-census growth rates from the 1996 and 2006 Livestock Censuses.
claims.actor: Ministry of National Food Security & Research
actors: Ministry of National Food Security & Research

claims.source_quote: calculated by applying milk production parameters to the projected population
claims.claim: Milk and meat production figures are calculated from production parameters applied to projected livestock populations based on inter-census growth rates.
claims.actor: Ministry of National Food Security & Research
actors: Ministry of National Food Security & Research

claims.source_quote: population growth, increases in per capita income and export opportunities are fueling the demand
claims.claim: Population growth, higher per capita income, and export opportunities are fueling demand for livestock and livestock products.
claims.cause: population growth, increases in per capita income and export opportunities
claims.effect: fueling the demand of livestock and livestock products

claims.source_quote: private sector-led development with public sector providing enabling environment
claims.claim: The livestock development strategy emphasizes private sector-led development with the public sector providing an enabling policy environment.
claims.effect: economic growth, food security and rural socioeconomic uplift

claims.source_quote: Poultry sector is one of the vibrant segments of livestock sector in Pakistan
claims.claim: The poultry sector is characterized as a vibrant livestock segment, employing over 1.5 million people and carrying more than Rs.700 billion in current investment.
claims.effect: employment to over 1.5 million people

claims.source_quote: Poultry meat contributes 31 percent of the total meat production in the country
claims.claim: Poultry meat contributed 31 percent of Pakistan's total meat production.

claims.source_quote: poultry value added at current factor cost has increased from Rs. 151.1 billion
claims.claim: Poultry value added at current factor cost increased by 7.7 percent in 2016-17 compared with the previous year.

claims.source_quote: continued regulatory measures that included allowing import of high yielding animals, semen and embryos
claims.claim: The Livestock Wing continued regulatory measures allowing imports of high-yielding animals, genetics, feed inputs, and livestock machinery to improve productivity and encourage value-added industry.
claims.actor: Livestock Wing of Ministry of NFS&R
actors: Livestock Wing of Ministry of NFS&R
claims.effect: genetic improvement of indigenous dairy animals and establishment of value added industry

claims.source_quote: A total of 41.286 thousand tons of red meat was exported during 2016-17
claims.claim: Pakistan exported 41.286 thousand tons of red meat during July-March 2016-17, fetching US$135.036 million from private-sector slaughterhouses.
claims.cause: Livestock Wing provided facilitation for export of red meat
claims.effect: export of meat fetched US $ 135.036 million

claims.source_quote: effort continued for market access with the concerned authorities of Russia, China, South Africa, Egypt, Hong Kong and Indonesia
claims.claim: Efforts continued through diplomatic channels to obtain market access for meat and meat products in Russia, China, South Africa, Egypt, Hong Kong, and Indonesia.
claims.policy_or_programme: market access efforts for meat and meat products
policy_or_programmes: market access efforts for meat and meat products
claims.effect: export of meat and meat products

comparison.comparator: Russia, China, South Africa, Egypt, Hong Kong and Indonesia

claims.geography: Russia; China; South Africa; Egypt; Hong Kong; Indonesia; Pakistan

claims.source_quote: 9,123 exotic dairy cows were imported
claims.claim: Imports of superior semen and exotic dairy cattle were regulated to improve indigenous dairy animals, with imported cows adding about 70 million tons of milk per annum to the commercial milk chain.
claims.indicator_or_subject: import of semen and exotic dairy cows
claims.policy_or_programme: genetic improvement of indigenous dairy animals
policy_or_programmes: genetic improvement of indigenous dairy animals
claims.cause: import of superior quality semen and high yielding exotic dairy cattle
claims.effect: added approximately 70 million tons of milk per annum in the commercial milk chain/system

claims.source_quote: duty free import of calf milk replacer & cattle feed premix was allowed
claims.claim: Duty-free imports of calf milk replacer, cattle feed premix, and machinery for livestock processing were allowed to support dairy farmers and value-added livestock processing.
claims.policy_or_programme: duty free import of calf milk replacer, cattle feed premix, and processing machinery
policy_or_programmes: duty free import of calf milk replacer
duty free import of cattle feed premix
duty free import of machinery for milk, beef, mutton and poultry processing
claims.effect: facilitate dairy farmers and promote value added livestock processing industry

claims.source_quote: AQD provided quarantine services and issued 28,217 Health Certificates
claims.claim: The Animal Quarantine Department provided quarantine services and issued 28,217 health certificates for livestock-related exports valued at US$294.414 million during July-March 2016-17.
claims.effect: export of live animals, mutton, beef, eggs and other livestock products

claims.source_quote: Pakistan has been progressing on OIE FMD freedom pathway and moved to stage 02
claims.claim: Pakistan progressed on the OIE Foot and Mouth Disease freedom pathway and moved to stage 2 of the six-stage pathway.
claims.policy_or_programme: National Programme to Control Foot & Mouth Disease (FMD) and Peste des Petitis Ruminants (PPR)
policy_or_programmes: National Programme to Control Foot & Mouth Disease (FMD) and Peste des Petitis Ruminants (PPR)
claims.cause: inter-provincial coordination and disease control activities
claims.effect: improving animal health status of the country regarding transboundary animal diseases

claims.source_quote: Government of UAE has lifted ban on import of poultry and poultry products from Pakistan
claims.claim: After inspection by a UAE technical team, the UAE government lifted its ban on poultry and poultry product imports from Pakistan, which was expected to promote exports.
claims.constraint_or_risk: ban on import of poultry and poultry products from Pakistan
claims.cause: receipt of inspection report from UAE technical team
claims.effect: promoting export of poultry and poultry products from the country

comparison.baseline: ban on import of poultry and poultry products from Pakistan

claims.source_quote: Royal Friesland Company acquired 51 percent of Engro Food Pakistan
claims.claim: Royal Friesland's 51 percent acquisition of Engro Food Pakistan represented one of Pakistan's largest private-sector foreign direct investments in dairy, amounting to US$450 million.
claims.effect: positive signal and more investments in dairy sector are expected

claims.source_quote: regulatory duties to the tune of 25 percent have been imposed
claims.claim: A 25 percent regulatory duty was imposed on imports of skimmed milk powder and whey powder to attract dairy investment, protect dairy farmers and corporate dairy, and discourage synthetic milk and recipe products.
claims.indicator_or_subject: regulatory duty on milk powder imports
claims.policy_or_programme: regulatory duties on Skimmed Milk Powder (SMP) and Whey Powder (WP)
policy_or_programmes: regulatory duties on Skimmed Milk Powder (SMP)
claims.constraint_or_risk: imports and use of synthetic milk and recipe products
claims.cause: attract further investment in dairy sector, protect small dairy farmers and corporate dairy sector, discourage import and mitigate use of synthetic milk and recipe products

claims.source_quote: Future Plans will continue to focus on i) Inter-Provincial Coordination
claims.claim: Future livestock plans focus on inter-provincial coordination, private-sector value addition, control of transboundary animal diseases, more investment, and new export markets for meat and dairy products.
claims.constraint_or_risk: Trans-boundary Animal Diseases (FMD, PPR, Zoonotic diseases)
claims.effect: development of livestock sector and export of meat and dairy products
claims.geography: Pakistan; Global Halal Food Trade Market

claims.source_quote: Fishery plays an important role in Pakistan’s economy
claims.claim: Fishery is described as important to Pakistan's economy and as a livelihood source for coastal inhabitants.
claims.indicator_or_subject: fisheries role in economy and livelihoods
claims.effect: source of livelihood for the coastal inhabitants
